message:
feat: expand discovery filter tests, connector introspect, and policy E2E coverage

Add comprehensive combined-dimension filter tests to discovery.rs
covering category+risk, category+risk+health, health-only, empty filter,
risk boundary, multi-category, and degraded health scenarios (360 lines).
Extend connector introspect tests with rate limit, resource quota, and
dependency chain assertions. Migrate policy simulation harness to use
emit_log_with_result for structured pass/fail logging and add policy
boundary, conflicting rules, and wildcard scope E2E tests. Update CBOR
test vectors for enrollment and simulation requests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -474,7 +474,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eqb","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide streaming access to Home Assistant’s event bus via WebSocket:
- state_changed
- call_service
- custom events

## Scope
- Establish websocket session and authenticate.
- Subscribe to events with filters (domain/entity allowlists).
- Deliver events as FCP2 event envelopes with:
  - stable event IDs
  - correlation IDs

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.events.subscribe` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned (WS + HTTPS to HA host only).
- Streaming must be bounded/backpressured:
  - cap queue depth
  - drop/deny with explicit reason codes when overloaded
- Taint:
  - event payloads are tainted external input

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `ha.events.subscribe`
- Event envelope types:
  - `ha.event.state_changed`
  - `ha.event.call_service`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - reconnect + resubscribe
  - bounds/backpressure behavior
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Event streaming is reliable under reconnects and never causes unbounded memory growth.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","title":"TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests (Availability Tracking, Pre-staging, Coverage Metrics)","description":"# TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive unit tests for offline capability tracking from V2 spec section 21.

## Test Categories

### OfflineAccess Tests
- can_access() returns true when local_symbols >= k
- can_access() returns false when local_symbols < k
- coverage() calculates correct ratio (local_symbols / k)
- Edge cases: k=0, local_symbols=0, overflow

### OfflineCapability Tests
- Accessible map tracks multiple objects correctly
- Object removal updates capability state
- Query by zone returns correct subset
- Memory efficient for large object counts

### Pre-staging Tests
- Access pattern tracking updates correctly
- Frequent objects prioritized for pre-staging
- Pre-staging respects device storage limits
- Stale access patterns decay appropriately
- Pre-staging triggers before predicted access

### Integration with Stores
- Symbol store updates reflect in OfflineAccess
- Symbol eviction updates can_access() correctly
- Repair loop improves coverage metrics

## Golden Vectors
- Location: tests/golden/offline_capability/
- Include: OfflineAccess serialization, coverage calculations

## Logging Requirements (per 1n78.35)
All tests emit structured JSON:
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test": "can_access_sufficient_symbols",
  "object_id": "...",
  "local_symbols": 60,
  "k": 50,
  "expected_can_access": true,
  "result": "pass|fail"
}

## Fuzz Targets
- OfflineAccess from arbitrary symbol counts
- Pre-staging priority calculation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All can_access/coverage calculations correct
- Pre-staging improves offline availability
- Integration with stores is consistent","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:41:38.187529965Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:42:07.112633034Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:42:07.112633034Z","close_reason":"26 comprehensive unit tests covering all requirements: OfflineAccess, OfflineCapability, AccessPatternTracker with edge cases","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f50","title":"fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting","description":"# fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable controlled Reddit participation:
- submit posts
- submit comments
- edit/delete own content

These are real-world side effects and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Submit post to a subreddit (text/link).
- Submit comment.
- Edit content.
- Delete content.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.post.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.comment.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.content.edit` (dangerous)

Policy guidance:
- Posting should often require ApprovalToken depending on zone policy.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Content safety:
  - outbound content must be treated as a “publication” action
  - receipts + audit events include subreddit + post/comment id
  - never log post bodies

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.post.submit`
  - `reddit.comment.submit`
  - `reddit.content.edit`
  - `reddit.content.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - request formation
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - error mapping (subreddit rules, forbidden)

## Acceptance criteria
- Write operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fbql","title":"TEST: Computation Migration Unit Tests (Checkpoint, Lease Transfer, Resume)","description":"# TEST: Computation Migration Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive unit and integration tests for computation migration from V2 spec section 16.

## Test Categories

### Checkpoint Tests
- Checkpoint creation serializes all computation state
- Checkpoint is content-addressed ObjectId
- Checkpoint can be reconstructed from symbols
- Large checkpoint is chunked correctly

### Lease Transfer Tests
- Execution lease transfers atomically during migration
- Duplicate execution prevented during migration window
- Lease transfer fails gracefully on network partition
- Fencing token is updated after transfer

### Resume Tests
- Target device reconstructs checkpoint correctly
- Computation resumes from checkpoint progress
- State transitions: Suspended -> Running are audited
- Failed resume transitions to Failed state correctly

### Adversarial Tests
- Migration during network partition (split brain prevention)
- Concurrent migrations of same computation (conflict resolution)
- Checkpoint corruption detection
- Lease expiry during migration

## Golden Vectors
- Location: tests/golden/computation_migration/
- Include: checkpoint serialization, lease transfer messages, resume protocol

## Logging Requirements (per 1n78.35)
All tests emit structured JSON:
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test": "checkpoint_creation",
  "computation_id": "...",
  "checkpoint_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error": "optional error message"
}

## Fuzz Targets
- Checkpoint deserialization (arbitrary bytes)
- Migration request parsing
- Lease transfer message validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All checkpoint creation/restoration tests pass
- Lease transfer is atomic (no duplicate execution)
- Adversarial scenarios handled correctly","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:41:13.221432187Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T08:41:28.383052588Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mesh","mvp","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fbql","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fbql","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6o25.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fbql","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-mhiv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ftw","title":"fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management","description":"# fcp.reddit: Saved Items Management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to manage saved posts/comments for research workflows:
- save/unsave
- list saved items

## Scope
- Save a post/comment.
- Unsave a post/comment.
- List saved items (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.saved.read`
- `reddit.saved.write` (dangerous-ish)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts/audit for write operations.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.saved.list`
  - `reddit.saved.save`
  - `reddit.saved.unsave`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - save/unsave idempotency
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Saved-items workflows are reliable and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-g1s","title":"fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts)","description":"# fcp.spotify: Search (Tracks, Albums, Artists, Podcasts) (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide fast, typed Spotify search for discovery workflows.

## Scope
- Search across types:
  - track
  - album
  - artist
  - playlist
  - show/podcast
  - episode
- Support filters:
  - market/locale
  - pagination

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.search.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Spotify Web API host.
- Treat titles/descriptions as tainted external content.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.search`
  - `spotify.search.tracks`
  - `spotify.search.albums`
  - `spotify.search.artists`
  - `spotify.search.shows`
  - `spotify.search.episodes`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - market handling
  - rate limiting + retry/backoff

## Acceptance criteria
- Search results are stable and correctly typed.
